insights: 14 of 20 rejections (70%) flagged as wrong_market_price; premium pricing on commons is primary rejection driver.
Facsimile signatures account for 2 rejections; legitimate autos in confirmations have proper authentication (Topps Tribute, Authentic Images).
Lot/multi-card listings rejected 3 times; confirmations are always single, specific cards.
Confirmation delta average: 81.65% (real snipes deeply underpriced vs. market); rejections lack pricing data suggesting overpriced positioning.
5 rejections (25%) contain mismatched title/card specs (wrong player, wrong set, wrong parallel); confirmations have exact title-card alignment.
Novelty, reprint, and custom parallel language appears only in rejections (0 in confirmations).
Griffey Base parallel dominates rejection pool (7 of 20), all overpriced; suggests 1989 UD #1 is high-vigilance target.
Confirmation titles use minimal marketing language; rejection titles use caps, emojis, 'RARE', 'INVESTMENT', 'MVP'—red flags for inflated pricing.
